title: A souvenir I didn't buy in Tunis
summary: A short reflection about an object you almost bought abroad and chose to leave behind.
topic: A souvenir I didn't buy in Tunis
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about something you almost bought abroad but decided not to. Describe the object, why you wanted it, and why you walked away. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: In the medina of Tunis, in a narrow alley I cannot find again, I almost bought…
I held it for almost ten minutes before I…
sampleText: In the medina of Tunis, in a narrow alley I cannot find again, I almost bought a small leather journal with a hand-stitched cover. It was the colour of dark honey and had a thin red ribbon as a bookmark. The owner of the tiny shop was an old man with very long fingers. He let me hold the journal for almost ten minutes without saying a single word. I opened every page, smelled the paper, and tried to imagine the book I might write inside it. While I was thinking, two other tourists came in, asked the price of something, and left quickly. I asked his price, and it was very fair. I had the money in my pocket. But somehow, that morning, I knew I did not want to write inside that book. It was already too beautiful for any sentence I had in me. I gave it back gently, thanked him, and walked out into the bright alley. Five years later, the journal still lives in the medina in my head, perfect and empty. Sometimes the things we do not buy stay with us longer than the things we do. The weight of the leather is still in my hand.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trong một con ngõ hẹp mà tôi không thể tìm lại được

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a small leather journal with a hand-stitched cover
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một cuốn sổ da nhỏ với bìa khâu tay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a thin red ribbon as a bookmark
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một dải ruy băng đỏ mỏng làm dấu trang

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He let me hold the journal for almost ten minutes
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông cho phép tôi cầm cuốn sổ gần mười phút

sampleAnnotations.phrase: tried to imagine the book I might write inside it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cố tưởng tượng cuốn sách mà tôi có thể viết bên trong
Trong khi tôi đang suy nghĩ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: It was already too beautiful for any sentence I had in me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Nó đã quá đẹp so với bất kỳ câu chữ nào tôi có trong mình

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I gave it back gently, thanked him, and walked out
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đưa nó lại một cách nhẹ nhàng, cảm ơn ông, và bước ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Sometimes the things we do not buy stay with us longer than the things we do
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Đôi khi những thứ chúng ta không mua ở lại với chúng ta lâu hơn những thứ chúng ta mua

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The weight of the leather is still in my hand
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trọng lượng của miếng da vẫn còn trong tay tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Let + object + base verb' (causative)
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'He let me hold the journal' dùng cấu trúc 'let + somebody + base verb' (không có 'to'). 'Allow' thì cần 'to': 'He allowed me to hold...'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'He let me hold the journal' uses 'let + somebody + base verb' (no 'to'). 'Allow' requires 'to': 'He allowed me to hold...'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Might' for past possibility
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'the book I might write inside it' — 'might' chỉ một khả năng tưởng tượng, không phải sự thật chắc chắn. Sau 'might' luôn là động từ nguyên mẫu.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The book I might write inside it' — 'might' marks an imagined possibility, not a certain fact. After 'might' use the base verb.

sampleTranslationVi: Trong khu phố cổ Tunis, trong một con ngõ hẹp mà tôi không thể tìm lại được, tôi gần như đã mua một cuốn sổ da nhỏ với bìa khâu tay. Nó có màu mật ong sẫm và có một dải ruy băng đỏ mỏng làm dấu trang. Chủ tiệm nhỏ là một ông cụ với ngón tay rất dài. Ông cho phép tôi cầm cuốn sổ gần mười phút mà không nói một từ nào. Tôi mở từng trang, ngửi giấy, và cố tưởng tượng cuốn sách mà tôi có thể viết bên trong. Trong khi tôi đang suy nghĩ, hai du khách khác bước vào, hỏi giá một món, và rời đi nhanh. Tôi hỏi giá của ông, và nó rất phải chăng. Tôi có tiền trong túi. Nhưng bằng cách nào đó, sáng hôm đó, tôi biết tôi không muốn viết bên trong cuốn sách ấy. Nó đã quá đẹp so với bất kỳ câu chữ nào tôi có trong mình. Tôi đưa nó lại một cách nhẹ nhàng, cảm ơn ông, và bước ra con ngõ sáng. Năm năm sau, cuốn sổ vẫn sống trong khu phố cổ trong đầu tôi, hoàn hảo và trống. Đôi khi những thứ chúng ta không mua ở lại với chúng ta lâu hơn những thứ chúng ta mua. Trọng lượng của miếng da vẫn còn trong tay tôi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence.
options: I almost bought a small leather journal with a hand-stitched cover.
I almost buyed a small leather journal with a hand-stitched cover.
I almost am buying a small leather journal with a hand-stitched cover.
I almost have bought a small leather journal with a hand-stitched cover.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'let + object + base verb' sentence.
options: He let me hold the journal for almost ten minutes.
He let me to hold the journal for almost ten minutes.
He let me holding the journal for almost ten minutes.
He let me held the journal for almost ten minutes.
the old walled part of a city in North Africa
a modern shopping mall
a small mountain village
an open beach
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Medina' = the historic walled district of a North African city.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While I was thinking, two other tourists came in.
While I thinking, two other tourists came in.
While I think, two other tourists came in.
While I am thinking, two other tourists came in.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was thinking' for ongoing background; past simple 'came in' for the inserted action.

options: the book I might write inside it
the book I might to write inside it
the book I might wrote inside it
the book I might writing inside it
more than the required amount, so that something is not possible
less than the required amount
exactly enough
almost enough
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Too + adjective + for + noun' = excessive in a way that prevents something.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about giving back.
options: I gave it back gently, thanked him, and walked out.
I gave back it gently, thanked him, and walked out.
I gave it back gentle, thanked him, and walked out.
I given it back gently, thanked him, and walked out.
mcQuestions.explanation: Separable phrasal verb 'give back' takes pronoun between parts ('give it back'); adverb 'gently' modifies the verb.

options: The things we do not buy stay with us longer than the things we do.
The things we do not buy stay with us more long than the things we do.
The things we do not buy stay with us longest than the things we do.
The things we do not buy stay with us long than the things we do.
to feel sorry about something you did or did not do
to feel very happy
to ask for help
to laugh quietly
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Regret' = to feel sorrow over a past decision.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'cannot find again'.
options: in a narrow alley I cannot find again
in a narrow alley I cannot to find again
in a narrow alley I cannot found again
in a narrow alley I am cannot find again

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing line.
options: The weight of the leather is still in my hand.
The weight of the leather is still on my hand.
The weight of the leather are still in my hand.
The weight of leather is still in my hand.
mcQuestions.explanation: Singular subject 'weight' → 'is'; 'in my hand' (inside the grip); 'the leather' (specific).

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do the things we choose not to buy sometimes haunt us more than the things we do? What does that say about the value of an object?
